meta.setting: Hay meadows, oak edges, winter ranges, deer trails, and elk country on the Jefferson frontier
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with hunting, food scarcity, predator draw, restraint, land claims, and hard choices

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Deer
research_grounding.use: Black-tailed deer and mule deer presence across California and their role as prey, food, and track sign.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Elk
research_grounding.use: California elk species, herd-scale landscape pressure, and northern/wider frontier context.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Mountain Lion

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Elk and Deer Herds cast page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented winter-count note, track warning, or meat-sharing saying generated later
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Meat, Tracks, and Winter Warnings
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing deer and elk as food, sign, trade pressure, predator draw, and weather indicators
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four grounded signs or behaviors
10 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable move, warning, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Generate deer and elk as living landscape systems: food, tracks, migration, predator draw, winter scarcity, and contested hunting.
Tie future entries to tracking, overhunting, game law, tribal food rights, ranch hay, herd panic, meat sharing, storm warning, and cougar or wolf sign nearby.
Keep animals naturalistic and avoid trophy-postcard framing.
Make restraint playable: the best choice may be watching, waiting, sharing, or leaving the herd alone.
Do not include final move names or final move text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: The herd should make the table think about hunger before it thinks about antlers. A full meadow can be a promise, a trap, or evidence that a storm is moving wrong.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A herd scene should ask who needs meat, who has the right to hunt, what predator follows, and whether winter has already started counting.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western animal-sign, hunting, and food language only.
Keep deer and elk naturalistic and ecologically grounded.
The generated page must include image path cast/images/elk-and-deer-herds.jpg.
No finished page prose, final entries, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred-invented, or fake-tribal framing
do not make the animals trophies, mascots, monsters, or symbols only
do not use modern wildlife gear, vehicles, plastic props, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about food, tracks, hunting restraint, predator draw, winter pressure, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
